diagnosisHandedIn.conclusion: The 15.8% site-wide checkout completion rate drop is driven by a 31.2% spike in payment failure rate on mobile credit-card transactions, concentrated in SP (São Paulo) mobile sessions, affecting ~1,180 customers and causing ~$18,900 in directly attributable lost revenue. This is the sole confirmed causal driver.

hypothesesConsidered.hypothesis: Mobile credit-card payment failure rate spike (0.035 → 0.046) is the primary and only confirmed driver of the checkout completion rate drop
hypothesesConsidered.reasoning: The 31.2% payment failure spike is precisely scoped to credit_card + mobile, matching the concentration of the conversion drop. ~1,180 customers directly blocked, ~$18,900 in blocked revenue. No competing hypothesis is supported.

diagnosisHandedIn.suggestedInvestigation: Confirm with payment processor whether an issuer-level or gateway-level degradation is affecting mobile credit-card auth in SP; also verify whether any recent SDK or provider change coincides.

recommendations.title: Reactivate & retarget the ~1,180 blocked SP mobile credit-card customers with a voucher-backed recovery campaign
recommendations.rationale: ~1,180 customers hit a payment failure on mobile credit-card in SP during the degradation window, leaving ~$18,900 in revenue on the table. A one-off broadcast to this precise cohort — offering a small voucher as an apology/incentive — can recover a significant share of those carts before intent cools. The existing 'Mobile shoppers in Sao Paulo' segment (seg-mobile-sp, 13,980) must be narrowed to customers who fired a payment_failure event in the affected period to avoid over-messaging.

recommendations.title: Activate the paused Checkout Recovery Scenario with a mobile credit-card payment-failure trigger
recommendations.rationale: The existing 'Checkout recovery sequence' scenario (scn-checkout-recovery) is currently paused and fires 2 hours after checkout without purchase. The diagnosis confirms the payment failure is mobile + credit_card specific. Reactivating this scenario — augmented with a payment_failure event trigger scoped to mobile/credit_card — means any future gateway degradation or issuer-level failure auto-triggers recovery outreach without manual intervention. This converts a reactive one-off fix into a permanent safety net.

recommendations.title: Run an A/B experiment: voucher vs. no-voucher in the SP mobile recovery flow to calibrate incentive cost
recommendations.rationale: The diagnosis does not indicate customers abandoned due to price sensitivity — they were blocked by a technical failure. A voucher may accelerate return-to-checkout but is not strictly necessary, meaning an untested blanket voucher offer could erode margins unnecessarily. A rapid A/B experiment on the ~1,180-customer recovery campaign (or on the reactivated scenario going forward) will reveal whether the voucher meaningfully lifts conversion versus a plain 'your payment failed, try again' message, allowing the merchant to make a data-driven call on incentive spend.
